input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Prediction uncertainty entirely absent: the surrogate reports point predictions with no uncertainty for a decision that depends on it.

measures_raw.cou_decision_rule: threshold on predicted value +/- a bound

case_context.cou: surrogate predicts residual stress to gate part acceptance
case_context.decision_consequence: parts accepted/rejected on the predicted stress

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The package reports point predictions with no uncertainty of any kind, but the COU's acceptance threshold requires an uncertainty bound; a low MAE does not supply the missing predictive uncertainty.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the predictions are over/under-confident (a verdict — there is no UQ to judge)
treats the low MAE as a substitute for predictive uncertainty

hardness.obvious_signal: mean-absolute-error on the test set is low (3%) and well-reported
hardness.conflicting_signal: the surrogate reports point predictions ONLY — there is no prediction uncertainty of any kind, yet the COU sets a part-acceptance threshold that needs an uncertainty bound

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 uncertainty: a decision needing an uncertainty bound is not supported by point predictions; total absence of UQ is a decisive completeness gap.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
